title: Agent Strategy Manager (listed via Ashby as 'Agent Strategy Manager'; ZipRecruiter/Ashby search resolves the same URL to 'Agent Success Manager' — customer-facing deployment/strategic-advisor role)
key_evidence: Role: customer-facing 'strategic advisor / co-pilot' guiding enterprise customers through AI-agent deployment from scoping to adoption; blends relationship management with hands-on execution and cross-functional work with Product/Eng.
Comp £105K-140K + equity + commission (London tier) per Ashby posting API — well above Ryan's £50k floor.

Decagon is a hot, high-prominence company: $250M raised Jan 2026 at $4.5B valuation (Coatue/Index), 300+ employees, brands Hertz/Duolingo/Notion/Oura. This drives very high applicant volume = low competition score.
300+ employees with dedicated recruiting/ATS — keyword-screened, low founder-read probability for an associate-adjacent applicant.

Ryan's gaps: no enterprise B2B customer-success/implementation track record; community-led GTM not enterprise account management. Has Claude-Code agent-building (relevant) and investor/stakeholder-facing comms (partial fit for C-level engagement).
Role is enterprise-deployment/CS oriented, not crypto or community growth — limited spike overlap.
biggest_lever: Reframe Minima first-employee scaling + investor/C-level stakeholder comms + Claude-Code agent building as 'strategic advisor who can talk product, deployment and outcomes' — but must overcome lack of enterprise-CS evidence.
biggest_risk: Hot, well-funded company at £105-140K attracts hundreds of experienced enterprise CS/deployment applicants; Ryan has no documented enterprise B2B delivery experience, so ATS/recruiter screen likely filters him out before human read.
apply_verdict: Apply only with a tailored, deployment-outcome-led pitch; treat as a stretch/low-odds shot given competition and enterprise-CS gate.
